title: [v4.6] fix: pull parma parsing for the /build compat ep
body:
# Changes

Fixing Docker client incompatibility where the `/build` endpoint cannot parse "1" as a boolean.

This PR makes the endpoint to accept string representing boolean which are booleanish by Go standard: string that can be parsed by `strconv.ParseBool()`.

The bug reproduction:
```
# Broken compatibility with the docker CLI
docker build . -t alpine-1000 --pull
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Error response from daemon: failed to parse query parameter 'pull': "1": invalid pull policy: "1"
```

```release-note
Correct parsing of the pull query parameter for the compat /build endpoint.
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *matejvasek* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.6/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
WTF? How could this be merged so quickly??? Cirrus CI was still running how can the bot merge this? cc @cevich

Looks like the bot responded to the `lgtm` faster than the `hold` label :disappointed: 

This is an unfortunate (long-standing and very annoying) complication arising from the bot's inability to synchronize with github's Checks API.  AFAIK it's still using the (very old) status API (which does not support Cirrus or github actions).
